questions.question: Java is a platform-independent language.
questions.explanation: Java code is compiled into bytecode, which can run on any system with a Java Virtual Machine (JVM).

questions.question: In Java, a class can inherit from multiple other classes.
questions.explanation: Java supports only single inheritance for classes. However, a class can implement multiple interfaces.

questions.question: All Java objects are stored on the 'stack'.
questions.explanation: In Java, objects are stored on the 'heap', while primitive variables and method references are stored on the 'stack'.

questions.question: The 'finally' block in exception handling always executes, whether an exception occurred or not.
questions.explanation: This block is typically used for cleanup code, like closing files or database connections.

questions.question: An 'Interface' in Java can contain instance variables.
questions.explanation: Interfaces can only contain constants (public static final) and abstract methods (or default/static methods).

questions.question: The 'super' keyword is used to refer to the immediate parent class.
questions.explanation: It is often used to call parent class constructors or overridden methods.
